{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/283662181?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/berkant-koc", "id": 283662181, "login": "berkant-koc", "node_id": "U_kgDOEOhXZQ", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/berkant-koc", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-47425", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:P/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:U"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-73", "name": "External Control of File Name or Path"}], "description": "## Summary

`EntryPoint::FromStr` in `rattler_conda_types` performs only `.trim()` on the `command` field before the linker joins it onto the install prefix and writes an executable Python script. A malicious `noarch:python` package can ship an `info/link.json` with an entry-point name containing `..`, `/`, `\`, or an absolute path; the resulting file is written outside the prefix (or clobbers an existing in-prefix entry-point such as `bin/pip`) with mode `0o775` on Unix and a copied launcher `.exe` on Windows. This affects the default install path of `pixi install`, `rattler-build`, some methods in `py-rattler`, and any other consumer of the `rattler` install crate; no flag or post-link-script opt-in is involved.

Resolved in https://github.com/conda/rattler/pull/2445, released in rattler 0.43.2.

## Affected

- Repository: https://github.com/conda/rattler
- Commit: `a0e61a33da8b9d6de712fab2a879fa9da977e6e3` (HEAD at audit time, 2026-05-13 release)
- Downstream consumers reached through the same code path: `prefix-dev/pixi` @ `e640477`
- pixi 0.69.0 and rattler-build 0.65.0 fix this issue

## Researcher

Berkant Koc <me@berkoc.com>

title: rattler has an entry-point path traversal in noarch:python install (arbitrary file write)
